channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US and Iran meet for ‘tense’ but ‘constructive’ ceasefire talks
item.description: Negotiators from the US and Iran wrapped a day of talks in Switzerland.

item.title: No pressure to pass the ball to Ronaldo in World Cup: Portugal’s Conceicao
item.description: Ronaldo has faced criticism after his performance in Portugal’s first match of the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Ebola closure cuts off a lifeline between DRC’s Goma and Rwanda
item.description: Health officials defend the restrictions, but traders say the measures have disrupted the flow of goods and income.

item.title: Cape Verde fight back for second World Cup draw 2-2 against Uruguay
item.description: Uruguay could need a win over Spain next weekend to avoid a second consecutive FIFA World Cup group-stage exit.

item.title: Egyptian football fans chant for Palestine in Vancouver
item.description: Egyptian fans gathered outside BC Place in Vancouver ahead of their World Cup match against New Zealand.

item.title: Iranians gather in Tehran to support team in critical draw against Belgium
item.description: Iranian fans in Tehran watched their team secure a 0-0 draw against Belgium at the World Cup in Los Angeles.

item.title: Iran war live: Mediators hail ‘encouraging progress’ in US-Tehran talks
item.description: US, Iran establish committee to oversee talks and a 'de-confliction cell' to end attacks in Lebanon, mediators say.

item.title: Football fans in Gaza gather to watch Spain-Saudi Arabia match
item.description: Palestinians in Gaza gathered to watch Spain's World Cup victory over Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Belgium see red in goalless World Cup draw with Iran in Los Angeles
item.description: Nathan Ngoy was sent off for hauling down Mehdi Taremi, whose first-half strike was ruled offside in close Group G game.

item.title: What are the end goals of Iran-US negotiations?
item.description: Professor and political analyst Mahjoub Zweiri breaks down what intentions behind US-Iran talks will result in.

item.title: Mourners gather to remember Lebanese conservationist killed by Israel
item.description: People gathered in Beirut in memory of beloved turtle conservationist Mona Khalil, killed in Israeli attack.

item.title: Remembering Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah’s life’s work
item.description: Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah dedicated his life to documenting life in Gaza before he was killed by Israel.

item.title: Serena Williams to play women’s singles at Wimbledon as wildcard entrant
item.description: The seven-time ladies' singles champion will make her Grand Slam return at Wimbledon, six years after her last appearance.

item.title: Displaced Lebanese inspect shattered Nabatieh after Israel halts attacks
item.description: Displaced residents returned to Nabatieh after Israel ordered a halt to fighting in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Colombia elections take place as Abelardo de la Espriella leads polls
item.description: Colombians are voting in an election where senator Ivan Cepeda is racing against Abelardo de la Espriella.

item.title: Japan fans celebrate 4-0 World Cup win in Tokyo
item.description: Fans gathered at Tokyo's Shibuya Crossing after Japan beat Tunisia 4-0 in a World Cup group-stage match.

item.title: Ramiro Valdes, one of Cuba’s last living revolutionary figures, dies at 94
item.description: Valdes was close ally of Castro brothers and helped found Cuba's notorious intelligence apparatus.

item.title: Europe heatwave scorches cities and wildlife
item.description: Europe heatwave scorches cities and wildlife

item.title: Lamine Yamal scores first World Cup goal as Spain thrash Saudi Arabia
item.description: Yamal makes a goal-scoring return for Spain as European champions get their World Cup campaign back on track.

item.title: What’s next in the Strait of Hormuz crisis?
item.description: Iranian armed forces say they've closed the vital waterway after Israeli attacks on Lebanon.

item.title: Ethiopian prime minister’s party easily wins parliamentary election
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize winner PM Abiy Ahmed will stay in power, as analysts warn of renewed conflicts in Ethiopia.

item.title: Khamenei’s MoU stance stirs debate as Iran and US meet in Switzerland
item.description: Supreme leader has expressed reservations about interim deal with US, but most top decision-makers back it.

item.title: Iran, US, Israel: Memorandum of misunderstanding?
item.description: Chances are slim for progress between Iran and the US, argues Drop Site News co-founder Jeremy Scahill.

item.title: The looming Super El Nino
item.description: A powerful El Nino is expected to peak in November that could threaten food supply and increase extreme weather events.

item.title: India’s ‘Cockroach’ movement camps out until education minister resigns
item.description: The viral 'Cockroach' movement's supporters occupy New Delhi's protest site, refusing to leave until minister resigns.
